data.title: AKVA group ASA starting buyback of up to 60,000 shares for employee share programme announced on Sept 23, 2025.
data.description: On September 23, 2025, AKVA group ASA (referred to as "AKVA group" or the "Company") disclosed their commencement of repurchasing a maximum of 60,000 shares belonging to the company. This initiative aims to utilize the shares within its employee share program.

data.title: Legislators in NY aim to impose energy tax on Bitcoin mining, potentially prompting miners to relocate due to high electricity costs.
data.description: Proposed legislation in New York is aiming to crack down on cryptocurrency miners due to their significant electricity consumption. The new laws could result in a large number of miners moving away from the state to avoid the strict regulations. This move comes as a response to the miners' high energy usage, which has been a growing concern. The potential exodus of miners could have a noticeable impact on the industry in the state, with estimates suggesting that it could affect hundreds of mining operations. The proposed laws are part of a broader effort to address the environmental and economic implications of cryptocurrency mining.

data.title: Quantum computers may revive lost Bitcoin by reverse-engineering private keys, jeopardizing holders’ security.
data.description: There is a growing concern that quantum computing may pose a threat to Bitcoin security by allowing hackers to reverse engineer private keys from publicly exposed ones. This development could potentially endanger the security of the millions of Bitcoin holders worldwide. With the rapid advancement in quantum computing technology, the ability to exploit vulnerabilities in Bitcoin's encryption techniques is becoming a real possibility. This has prompted experts to suggest the need for enhanced security measures to safeguard against such risks in the future.

data.title: Bernie Sanders criticizes Trump for Blue State funding cuts, calls it unconstitutional. Warns of health insurance catastrophe.
data.description: Senator Bernie Sanders from Vermont has condemned President Donald Trump's handling of the government shutdown, stating that the situation could lead to a health insurance disaster. Sanders likened Trump's actions to a crisis similar to the Civil War, describing it as possibly the most severe America has faced since that time. In an interview with MSNBC, Sanders accused Trump of unconstitutional and illegal behavior, particularly in withholding federal funds from states that did not support him, which he labeled as illegal, outrageous, and unconstitutional. This news was reported on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Republican Senator Cruz stops bipartisan bill protecting Americans from data sales citing law enforcement concerns.
data.description: A Texas Republican recently pulled the plug on a bipartisan initiative designed to safeguard Americans from commercial data exploitation, citing concerns related to law enforcement. This decision, made unilaterally, has disrupted progress on legislation that aimed to enhance privacy and security measures for individuals. The move has resulted in a setback for efforts to address the increasing threat of data misuse, despite the collaborative efforts of lawmakers from different political parties. This action has raised criticism and questions regarding the prioritization of concerns surrounding data protection and the impact on citizens. The implications of this derailment have left many questioning the future of safeguarding sensitive information in a rapidly evolving digital landscape.
